name: viewermetrics
readme_summary: The Viewer Metrics Chrome Extension tracks and analyzes live stream viewers, differentiating between genuine users and potential bot accounts. It provides detailed analytics, user profiles, and visual charts to help streamers understand their audience composition.
key_features: Track viewer counts and authenticated users
Visual charts of legitimate vs. bot distribution
primary_use_case: Analyzing live stream viewership to identify and filter out bot accounts, providing streamers with accurate audience metrics.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant digital gardener tending to a vibrant garden representing a live stream. The gardener uses a special magnifying glass, labeled 'Viewer Metrics', to inspect each plant (viewer). Healthy, real plants glow with a soft, natural light, while suspicious, artificial-looking plants are clearly marked with a subtle red digital glitch effect. The gardener is carefully pruning the artificial plants, allowing only the genuine ones to flourish. In the background, a small, clean UI element shows a pie chart with 'Real Viewers' and 'Bots' clearly distinguished. The scene is set in a bright, modern digital space with subtle code elements integrated into the garden paths. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a focus on the contrast between organic and artificial elements.

name: dock-droid
description: Docker Android - Run QEMU Android in a Docker! X11 Forwarding! CI/CD for Android!
readme_summary: Dock-Droid allows users to run Android x86 and ARM environments within Docker containers using QEMU and KVM. It facilitates advanced use cases like security research, CI/CD pipelines, and app testing with features like X11 forwarding, ADB access, and GPU passthrough.
key_features: Run QEMU Android x86 and ARM in Docker
Magisk, riru, LSPosed support on Android x86
Support for BlissOS, custom images, VDI files, any Android x86 image, Xvfb headless mode
primary_use_case: Running and testing Android environments within Docker containers for development, security research, and CI/CD pipelines.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist in a futuristic laboratory, meticulously assembling a miniature, glowing Android robot from various digital components and code fragments. The alchemist is using a holographic interface to control the process, with streams of data flowing from a Docker whale icon into the Android. The background shows a stylized server rack with subtle code snippets and a 'Dock-Droid' logo integrated into the lab's design. The scene should convey the idea of creating and running a functional Android environment within a contained digital space. 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant neon accents and a clean, organized composition.

name: socktop
description: socktop is a remote system monitor with a rich TUI interface, inspired by `top` and `btop`, that communicates with a lightweight remote agent over WebSockets.
readme_summary: Socktop is a remote system monitoring tool that provides a rich TUI interface, inspired by `top` and `btop`. It communicates with a lightweight agent over WebSockets, offering detailed system metrics and process information.
key_features: Remote monitoring via WebSocket (JSON over WS)
Optional WSS (TLS) with client certificate pinning
Detailed CPU, Memory, Swap, Disk, Network, and Temperature metrics
primary_use_case: Remote system monitoring with a user-friendly TUI.
cover_image_prompt: A split scene: on the left, a complex, abstract network of glowing data streams flows from a small, unobtrusive server icon. On the right, a vibrant, detailed TUI dashboard materializes from these streams, displaying colorful graphs, process lists, and system metrics like CPU and memory usage. A subtle, stylized sock icon is integrated into the dashboard's header, representing the 'socktop' name. The overall aesthetic is clean, modern, and illustrative, with a focus on the transformation of raw data into understandable information. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a futuristic, digital theme and a clear, bright color palette.

name: Cronicle
description: A simple, distributed task scheduler and runner with a web based UI.
readme_summary: Cronicle is a robust, multi-server task scheduler and runner written in Node.js, offering a web-based UI for managing scheduled and on-demand jobs. It provides features like automated failover, real-time job status, live log viewing, and the ability to write plugins in any language.
key_features: Real-time job status with live log viewer
Plugins can be written in any language
Track CPU and memory usage for each job
Simple REST API for scheduling and running events
primary_use_case: A distributed task scheduler and runner with a web-based UI, serving as an advanced replacement for traditional cron jobs across multiple servers.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor orchestrating a symphony of diverse instruments, each representing a different server or task. The conductor, holding a glowing baton (representing the primary server), directs streams of musical notes (representing jobs) to various sections of the orchestra. Some instruments are highlighted with success glows, while others show subtle indicators of processing. Small, stylized UI elements like a clock and a progress bar are subtly integrated into the conductor's podium. The scene is set on a grand stage with dramatic lighting, emphasizing the synchronized execution of tasks. The overall style is a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a clear visual hierarchy, conveying organized automation and distributed control.

name: web-llm
readme_summary: WebLLM is a high-performance inference engine that runs Large Language Models (LLMs) directly in web browsers using WebGPU for hardware acceleration. It offers full compatibility with the OpenAI API, enabling local execution of various open-source models with features like streaming and JSON mode, all without requiring server support.
primary_use_case: To enable high-performance, privacy-preserving LLM inference directly within web browsers, leveraging local hardware acceleration (WebGPU) and offering OpenAI API compatibility for a wide range of open-source models.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot sitting comfortably at a desk in a sunlit room, holding a tablet that displays a chat interface. The robot is effortlessly conjuring complex ideas and text from the tablet, which are visualized as glowing, colorful streams of data flowing directly from the device into the robot's 'mind' (represented by a subtle, glowing aura around its head). Small, abstract representations of various LLM models (like stylized brains or gears) are visible in the background, connected to the tablet by thin, digital threads. The overall scene should convey ease of use, local processing power, and the magic of AI running directly on a personal device. The style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a bright and optimistic color palette.
